updated_conclusion: Function 0x14066DC10 is a shared worker-name registration/update helper used by Js5WorkerThread, MixerWorker, and VorbisWorker paths. It is not the worker-thread entry point itself; it accepts a parent/registry object in RCX, a NUL-terminated worker name in RDX, and a caller-provided two-qword record in R8, then validates/inserts or replaces a ref-counted pair under the RCX object.

tooling_milestone.change: Direct CALL rel32 hits now include containing .pdata function metadata when available.
tooling_milestone.why_it_matters: The 0x14066DC10 fan-in only becomes useful when each callsite is grouped by its owning function, revealing paired prelude/setup calls for each worker name.

evidence_source: GhidraCallsiteArgsPy scratch output for the six direct callsites
GhidraDumpWindowsPy scratch output for 0x14066DC10 and its caller windows

body_observations: Reads RDX as a NUL-terminated string and advances over it before calling 0x140031B10, consistent with measuring/copying a name.
Copies the caller-provided R8 pair into stack locals: [R8] and [R8+8] become the candidate ref-counted object/payload pair.
Calls 0x14066E030 with RDX=original RCX parent object and R8=stack pair; failure causes a false return.
On success, checks/locks RCX+0x20, calls 0x14066DDF0 with RCX+0x70 and a dword from [R8+8]+0x20, then installs/replaces the pair through a pointer reached from [R8+8].
Performs paired ref-count release calls through vtable slots +0x8 and +0x10 for replaced objects, then returns AL=1.

relationship_to_previous_milestones.js5-worker-provider-cluster-lifecycle: Explains the helper behind the Js5WorkerThread setup call at 0x14058F173.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.provider-descriptor-setup-function-liter: Turns the Js5WorkerThread label into a three-worker registration-family pattern.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.pe-runtime-functions: Direct-call results now reuse .pdata ownership so call fan-ins can be grouped without a separate runtime-function command.

bounded_inference.statement: 0x14066DC10 is best named worker-name registration helper or worker record publisher. It likely binds a named worker channel to a ref-counted worker/payload pair on a parent object, but the exact container type and validation performed by 0x14066E030 still need tracing.
bounded_inference.confidence: high for direct callers, worker-name literals, function ownership, and argument shapes at the setup calls; medium for the registration/update role; low for the exact container type

next_targets: Trace 0x14066E030 to identify the validation/search path used before pair installation.
Trace 0x14066DDF0 to identify the RCX+0x70 container and its key/value layout.
Trace 0x14001C520-0x14001CDD1, which references client, Js5WorkerThread, MixerWorker, and VorbisWorker together in the earlier bootstrap routine.
Promote GhidraCallsiteArgsPy-style argument setup extraction into a native PE callsite-args command once the byte-pattern decoder can cover the observed LEA/MOV stack-pair forms.
